latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

Share SEDOL:
BYRGPD6
TIDM: ALF

As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is a UK‑based investment vehicle that holds a small portfolio of real‑estate and infrastructure assets. It mainly earns income through rental yields and modest capital appreciation of those holdings.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The real‑estate sector continues to offer stable, low‑volatility cash flows, and Allanfield’s niche assets give it a defensible position within that market. Its portfolio is small enough that a single asset can materially affect performance, but large enough to generate regular dividend payouts for shareholders.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been actively returning capital to its shareholders through share buybacks and dividend payments, signalling a focus on liquidity rather than growth. Its share price sits far below the value implied by the assets it holds.

rns_narrative: The most recent filings show a steady stream of capital‑return activities: the firm has paid out cash to shareholders, refreshed its portfolio, and updated its net‑asset‑value figures. There is no sign of new acquisitions or expansion plans.
Collectively, the announcements paint a picture of a business that is busy consolidating and distributing rather than expanding.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: The market is largely silent, with only a handful of posts that echo the company’s own statements.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There is no bullish chatter – the few posts that exist simply repeat the company’s own messaging about returning capital.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish voices point to the absence of growth catalysts and the risk that the firm may be a thin‑margin vehicle that will struggle to sustain returns.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has moved from neutral to indifferent; no new excitement is building.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning there is no current search interest that could create a surprise event.

near_term_catalysts: An announcement of a new asset purchase or a strategic partnership could inject fresh optimism.
A scheduled share‑buyback that increases the price per share could serve as a trigger.
A change in the net‑asset‑value that shows a significant rise in underlying property values would be a positive signal.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in trading volume, a breakout above the recent support level, or a press release that announces a new investment opportunity.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in a late‑stage washout; the next meaningful move will likely come only after a clear catalyst or a change in the company’s capital‑return policy.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the firm announces a new asset or a share‑buyback, the price could rise sharply because the market has not priced in any future upside. A deep crash offers a 2‑to‑3‑fold potential return once the price moves back towards historical levels.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a new catalyst, the company may simply continue to distribute cash and stay at a low valuation. The risk of the share remaining flat or falling further is real if the asset base does not improve.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of roughly 200% versus a downside risk of about 80% if the price stays near its current level.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear volume spike that confirms a move above the recent low. Use a stop just below the last swing low and size the position to keep exposure to the upside within a manageable portion of your portfolio.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a share‑buyback announcement or a positive net‑asset‑value update. A price breakout above the 20‑day moving average could also be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None identified at present; the only terminal risk is that the company may remain a thin‑margin distribution vehicle.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield is a deeply discounted, low‑sentiment stock that could deliver a 2‑to‑3‑fold return if a capital‑return or asset‑purchase catalyst appears, but it remains vulnerable to staying flat if no new growth is introduced.
